reasoning: As of October 16, 2025, the Wikipedia article "List of infectious diseases" listed the status of a vaccine for Gonorrhea as "No". The development and official approval of a new vaccine is a lengthy process involving extensive clinical trials and regulatory review. Even though some MenB vaccines (like Bexsero) have shown cross-protection against gonorrhea and are under investigation, there is currently no specific or officially approved vaccine for gonorrhea. The timeline for a new vaccine to be approved and subsequently for Wikipedia to be updated reflecting a "Yes" status typically spans years, not the 1 to 3 months covered by the resolution dates (up to January 24, 2026). Given the short forecast horizons, the probability of this changing remains extremely low. I have slightly increased the probability for the furthest date to account for any unexpected rapid developments or Wikipedia editorial policy changes.
